---
title: |
  <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; gap:15px;">
    <img src="637382017185780333+290935.avif" style="height:70px;">
    <span>GP3 - Product Feed / Retail Media Setup</span>
  </div>
author: "Group 5: Luis Ahumada, Spencer Conway, Carmen Jimenez Rodriguez, Leeann Lewis-Waddell, & Georgia Monjarrez"
date: today
format: 
  html: 
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    number-sections: true
    code-link: true
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
execute: 
  freeze: auto
---

# Product feed spreadsheet

# Short retail-media setup explanation

# Feed-quality diagnosis

# Retail-media Strategy

The CPP Farm Store should use Google Merchant Center as its primary retail media channel because it is designed to improve product visibility across Google Search and Google Shopping. A complete and well organized product feed allows customers to discover products more easily while providing accurate information such as pricing, availability, product descriptions, and images. This increases the likelihood of attracting shoppers and generating both online interest and in store visits.

## **Products to Promote First**

The products that should be promoted first are the Farm Store’s signature and best-selling items, such as locally made jams, honey, fresh baked goods, and specialty gift baskets. These products represent the Farm Store’s brand, appeal to a wide audience, and are more likely to generate customer interest through online search and shopping ads.

## **Seasonal Campaigns**

Seasonal promotions should focus on products that align with holidays and local harvests. Gift baskets can be featured during the holiday season, while fresh fruits, vegetables, and seasonal baked goods can be promoted throughout the year as they become available. Highlighting seasonal products creates urgency and encourages repeat visits.

## **Products for Gift Buyers**

Gift baskets, specialty jams, local honey, and other packaged food items are ideal for gift buyers because they are easy to purchase, locally produced, and suitable for holidays, birthdays, or thank you gifts. These products should include high quality images and detailed descriptions to showcase their value.

## **Products That May Attract Students**

Affordable snacks, coffee, fresh produce, and ready to eat bakery items are likely to appeal to Cal Poly Pomona students. These products are convenient, reasonably priced, and relevant to students looking for quick meals or study snacks. Promotions targeting students can emphasize freshness, convenience, and campus accessibility.

## **Local Shopping and Store Pickup**

Fresh produce, baked goods, dairy products, and other perishable items are well suited for local shopping and store pickup. Promoting these products through Google Merchant Center allows nearby customers to see current availability and encourages them to visit the Farm Store in person. Including clear pickup information and accurate inventory helps create a better customer experience and supports local purchasing.

## **Overall Strategy**

A complete and accurate product feed improves search visibility and prepares the CPP Farm Store for future retail media campaigns. By prioritizing popular products, seasonal offerings, student friendly items, and products available for local pickup, the Farm Store can increase online exposure while driving more traffic to its physical location.

# Recommendation for which products should be promoted first

# Appendix

-   [GitHub Repo](https://github.com/lewiswaddell/GP3.git)

-   [GitHub Page](https://lewiswaddell.github.io/GP3/)
